tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Blood chloride is high and CO₂ is low, indicating possible metabolic acidosis. Urine is cloudy, brown, with bilirubin and ketones present, but no leukocytes or nitrites, making infection less likely. HbA1c is low, excluding chronic hyperglycemia. Findings are consistent with chronic kidney disease, possibly with hepatic involvement. Recommend nephrology evaluation.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
